tuning_key.text: We don't get to decide what will be on the other side / But I want to know I tried / As I move past the passing tide
tuning_key.attribution: Chords of Truth — Lucid Principles Canon (CC BY 4.0)

context_block: There is a steadiness beneath the noise that remains even when the tide rises to your chest. You don't need to know what waits on the other side to let the water move through you; you only need to stop clinging long enough to feel the shore that you are. Let what wants to leave, leave—what stays is the peace that has been waiting underneath all along.

practice.title: Feel the Hold
practice.instruction: Notice exactly where your body presses against the chair, floor, or earth beneath you, and let that solid pressure remind you that something remains steady even as everything else moves.

practice.title: Let the Tide Pass
practice.instruction: Take one slow breath in, then exhale as if the air is water carrying sediment outward, allowing whatever tension or thought wants to leave to travel out with it completely.

practice.title: Anchor the Echo
practice.instruction: At the bottom of the exhale, hold the silence for three seconds and let the echo audio phrase "steady as the earth beneath it" become the literal felt sensation of your weight arriving deeper into the support below.
